mnemonics: Wide Columns are for CAKES: Column-oriented, Analytics, Key-Value store style
keyPoints: Stores data by columns instead of rows, optimizing for aggregate queries and analytics.
Uses a flexible schema where each row can have its own set of columns.
Scales horizontally across distributed systems to handle massive datasets.
commonMistakes: Confusing them with Relational Database tables, which have a fixed schema for every row.
Assuming they are the same as Document Stores, which store data in JSON-like documents rather than sparse, columnar families.
